Very impressive to look at. We really enjoyed the small exhibit inside as well. The lady volunteering there was very friendly and knowledgeable; we enjoyed talking with her. The exhibit is not too big / not too long to visit, so it was a nice break from all the tourists outside (we were alone in there). The exhibit is in a wide room, with plenty of space to walk around, isolated from the noise outside, with big windows to let the sun shines in. Honestly, it was a great improvised stop! If you are interested by the topic, call in advance to ask when the veteran is present to add-in with great explanations of his personal experience in a submarine.

Just visited the USS Pampanito this morning. No crowd. The guy at the ticket booth was friendly. It's interesting, but very pricey at $50+ for two people! We didn't rush at all and we explored the entire sub in under 30 minutes. The visitors center was also closed for a private educational event. My son and I left feeling a bit underwhelmed.

The USS Pampanito is a submarine that was used in the Pacific against Japan during World War II. Today it is anchored in the Fisherman's Wharf waterfront district and can be visited. However, one should not have claustrophobia for the narrow spaces inside.

The mobile audio guide only works with an internet connection. Unfortunately, there is no Wi-Fi. Much more exciting than the audio guide, however, was the friendly older veteran on board. He had served decades ago on a submarine of the same type and could answer all my questions with great expertise. It was a real honour to meet a contemporary witness in this historic Mood and learn his story.

The only downside, besides the non-functioning audio guide, is the high entrance fee of 25 USD. That's fine with the contemporary witness, but without him it would definitely have been too expensive for me.
